Quality Education.reason: Focus on improving English proficiency and critical engagement aligns with Target 4.4 ('relevant skills for employment').
The course is a form of tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops skills for global citizenship and cultural diversity appreciation (Target 4.7).
evidence: College English I/II is a two-semester course, designed to help students improve their English language proficiency
be prepared for learning in a higher education environment in English
acquired familiarity with a selection of issues related to cross-cultural communication

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course develops communication skills and prepares students for internships, which are relevant for future employment and decent work (Targets 8.5, 8.6).
evidence: Prepare a statement of purpose for student exchange program, scholarship or internship
H. 國際移動能力International Mobility

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course explicitly aims to improve intercultural awareness and critique globalization, which relates to understanding and reducing inequalities between groups (Target 10.2).
evidence: Analyze cultural differences using relevant intercultural theories

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Course topics like "Gallery visit" and "Treasures of the Titanic" tangentially relate to safeguarding the world's cultural heritage (Target 11.4).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The focus on "cross-cultural communication" and "International Mobility" develops skills foundational for building global partnerships for sustainable development (Target 17.16).
